title: Bootstrap Essentials
description: Building responsive, mobile-first UIs with Bootstrap 4.

sections.title: Bootstrap Forms
sections.lesson:
What are Bootstrap Forms?

Bootstrap forms are pre-styled and responsive HTML form components designed to make form creation faster and easier. They use the .form-control, .form-group, and grid classes to improve layout and consistency.

Basic Form Structure

Bootstrap forms use .form-group to group labels and controls, and .form-control for input styling.

Example:

<form>
  <div class="form-group">
    <label for="email">Email address</label>
    <input type="email" class="form-control" id="email" placeholder="Enter email">
  </div>
  <div class="form-group">
    <label for="password">Password</label>
    <input type="password" class="form-control" id="password" placeholder="Password">
  </div>
  <button type="submit" class="btn btn-primary">Submit</button>
</form>

Output:

Horizontal Forms

Horizontal forms align labels and input fields side-by-side using the Bootstrap grid system.

<form>
  <div class="form-group row">
    <label for="name" class="col-sm-2 col-form-label">Name</label>
    <div class="col-sm-10">
      <input type="text" class="form-control" id="name" placeholder="Full Name">
    </div>
  </div>
</form>

Inline Forms

Use .form-inline for compact forms such as search bars or login sections.

<form class="form-inline">
  <input class="form-control mr-sm-2" type="search" placeholder="Search">
  <button class="btn btn-outline-success my-2 my-sm-0" type="submit">Search</button>
</form>

Form Validation

Bootstrap provides validation styles using .was-validated and HTML5 validation attributes.

<form class="needs-validation" novalidate>
  <div class="form-group">
    <label for="username">Username</label>
    <input type="text" class="form-control" id="username" required>
    <div class="invalid-feedback">Please enter your username.</div>
  </div>
  <button type="submit" class="btn btn-primary">Submit</button>
</form>
Please enter your username.

Custom Form Controls

Bootstrap also includes custom styles for checkboxes, radio buttons, and file inputs.

<div class="custom-control custom-checkbox">
  <input type="checkbox" class="custom-control-input" id="customCheck1">
  <label class="custom-control-label" for="customCheck1">Remember me</label>
</div>

Summary

  • Use .form-group and .form-control for consistent spacing and input styling.
  • Leverage the grid system to build horizontal forms.
  • Use .form-inline for compact forms.
  • Enhance usability with Bootstrap’s validation and custom controls.

References

  • W3Schools: Bootstrap Forms
  • Bootstrap 4 Documentation: Forms
  • MDN: HTML Forms

sections.title: Bootstrap Tables
sections.lesson:
What are Bootstrap Tables?

Bootstrap provides built-in classes that style tables with consistent spacing, borders, hover effects, striped rows, and responsive layouts. These styles are applied using simple class names like .table, .table-striped, .table-hover, and .table-responsive.

Basic Table

A basic Bootstrap table uses the .table class.

Example:

<table class="table">
  <thead>
    <tr>
      <th>#</th>
      <th>First</th>
      <th>Last</th>
      <th>Handle</th>
    </tr>
  </thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr>
      <th>1</th>
      <td>Mark</td>
      <td>Otto</td>
      <td>@mdo</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <th>2</th>
      <td>Jacob</td>
      <td>Thornton</td>
      <td>@fat</td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

Output:

# First Last Handle
1 Mark Otto @mdo
2 Jacob Thornton @fat

Striped Tables

Add .table-striped to alternate row background colors.

<table class="table table-striped">...</table>
# First Last Handle
1 Anna Smith @anna
2 Carl Johnson @cj

Hoverable Rows

Use .table-hover to add a hover highlight when the mouse moves over rows.

<table class="table table-hover">...</table>
# Title Author Year
1 Learning C J. Doe 2020
2 Web Dev Guide M. Ray 2021

Responsive Tables

Wrap tables inside .table-responsive so they become scrollable on small screens.

<div class="table-responsive">
  <table class="table">...</table>
</div>
# Device OS Version Status Notes
1 Phone A Android 10 Active Test Device
2 Phone B iOS 14 Inactive Needs Update

Summary

  • Use .table for basic table styling.
  • Apply .table-striped or .table-hover for readability.
  • Wrap with .table-responsive for mobile-friendly layouts.

References

  • W3Schools: Bootstrap Tables
  • Bootstrap 4 Documentation: Tables
